title: Panama Canal tolls see sharp surge amid Strait of Hormuz Blockade
summary:
Transit fees in the Panama Canal have risen since the Middle East conflict and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz began, according to the Panama Canal Authority. 
The canal has witnessed increased d

sources.title: Panama Canal tolls see sharp surge amid Strait of Hormuz Blockade
sources.excerpt:
Transit fees in the Panama Canal have risen since the Middle East conflict and the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz began, according to the Panama Canal Authority. 
The canal has witnessed increased demand to move vital cargo. 
The average price in an auction before the conflict was in the $135,000-$140,000 range. 
However, since the conflict began, this rose to as much as $385,000 during March and April.

sources.title: UK manufacturing sentiment falls to multi-year low as output dips: CBI

UK manufacturing sentiment has sharply deteriorated, with output and orders declining and competitiveness weakening, according to the CBI.  
New orders fell at the fastest pace since 2020, while costs rose faster than selling prices, squeezing margins.

Rising energy costs and Middle East tensions are intensifying uncertainty.

title: India expected to breach FY27 budgeted fiscal deficit target: BMI
summary: India's fiscal deficit is likely to breach the FY27 budgeted target and hit 4.5 per cent of GDP as the government's policy response to the Middle East conflict could strain public finances, according

sources.title: India expected to breach FY27 budgeted fiscal deficit target: BMI
sources.excerpt:
India's fiscal deficit is likely to breach the FY27 budgeted target and hit 4.5 per cent of GDP as the government's policy response to the Middle East conflict could strain public finances, according to data analytics firm BMI. 
The FY27 budget had projected a 4.3-per cent fiscal deficit. 
BMI expects the government to introduce policies to restrain business costs and improve financial support for firms.

Its cumulative offshore wind installed capacity has surpassed 47 million kW, leading the world for fi

sources.title: China boosts offshore wind capacity to speed up low-carbon transition
sources.excerpt:
China is developing several advanced offshore wind projects, fast-tracking its energy transition. 
Its cumulative offshore wind installed capacity has surpassed 47 million kW, leading the world for five consecutive years. 
China is now shifting its focus to deeper, more distant waters. 
It has also developed a robust, clustered offshore wind industrial supply chain, with key hubs in coastal provinces.

title: FY27 Bangladesh inflation to be above central bank target at 8.6%: BMI
summary:
Inflation in Bangladesh is likely to reach 8.6 per cent in FY27 due to higher energy prices triggered by the Iran war, according to data analytics firm BMI. 
Inflation may remain above the Bangladesh

sources.title: FY27 Bangladesh inflation to be above central bank target at 8.6%: BMI
sources.excerpt:
Inflation in Bangladesh is likely to reach 8.6 per cent in FY27 due to higher energy prices triggered by the Iran war, according to data analytics firm BMI. 
Inflation may remain above the Bangladesh Bank’s 6.5-per cent target set in its latest monetary policy, the Fitch Solutions company said. 
The central bank may keep the policy rate unchanged at 10 per cent in FY27 instead of cutting it, BMI said.

North India cotton yarn prices remained steady as mills continued to push rates higher, supported by strong export orders and healthy margins.

The project, expected to create aroun

sources.title: Egypt’s SCZONE signs $16.5-mn pact with Turkish firm for RMG project
sources.excerpt:
The Suez Canal Economic Zone has signed a $16.5-million pact with Turkish firm ELA Tekstil to set up a RMG and denim factory in the Qantara West Industrial Zone. 
The project, expected to create around 2,500 direct jobs, will produce nearly 7 million pieces of RMG and denim products annually, with four-fifths of output meant for exports. 
This project brings the total number of projects in the Zone to 42.

title: Norway sees steady apparel imports at $389 mn in Jan–Feb period

Cotton and man-made fibres dominated mate

sources.title: Norway sees steady apparel imports at $389 mn in Jan–Feb period
sources.excerpt:
Norway’s apparel imports remained stable at $389.96 million in January-February 2026.
Unisex garments led demand, with trousers and shorts topping categories.
Cotton and man-made fibres dominated materials, while woven garments held a higher share than knits.
China remained the top supplier, Bangladesh steady, and Turkiye’s share declined, indicating a slight sourcing shift.

Textile yarn and fabric i

Full-year 2025 exports remained under pressure, extending a post-pandemic correction. 
Q4 data indicates only a partial sequential rebound.
